title: Argentinian Tofu Empanadas
description: A twist on traditional argentinian empanadas, filled with marinated tofu and served with a side of pungent chimichurri.

ingredients.name: Tofu
ingredients.notes: Marinated in a mixture of olive oil, garlic, and oregano

ingredients.name: Empanada dough
ingredients.notes: Available at most grocery stores or made from scratch

ingredients.name: Pungent chimichurri
ingredients.notes: For serving, made with oregano, parsley, garlic, red pepper flakes, red wine vinegar, and olive oil

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Used to season the filling and dough

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Used to season the filling and dough

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Used to brush the empanadas before baking

instructions: Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the marinated tofu in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with olive oil.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve the empanadas with marinara sauce and pungent chimichurri.
